title: fagus sylvatica f. purpureopendula
intro: Fagus sylvatica f. purpureopendula, also known as Purple Copper Beech, is a deciduous tree that belongs to the Fagaceae family, characterized by its copper-colored leaves with long, slender petioles. The leaves of the Purple Copper Beech contain tannins, which are known to have medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Fagus sylvatica f. purpureopendula include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
